title: Decree of the Ministry of Transport and Communications implementing the Road Act

parts.PÉČE VLASTNÍKA O KOMUNIKACE A JEJICH EVI: OWNER'S MAINTENANCE OF ROADS AND THEIR REGISTRATION
parts.ROZSAH, ZPŮSOB A ČASOVÉ LHŮTY PRO ODSTRA: SCOPE, METHOD AND TIME LIMITS FOR REMOVING DEFECTS IN ROAD USABILITY
parts.STYK KOMUNIKACÍ S VODNÍMI TOKY, DRÁHAMI,: INTERSECTION OF ROADS WITH WATERCOURSES, RAILWAYS, UTILITY NETWORKS AND OTHER LINES
parts.TECHNICKÉ PODMÍNKY PRO PŘIPOJOVÁNÍ KOMUN: TECHNICAL CONDITIONS FOR CONNECTING ROADS AND ADJACENT PROPERTIES TO A ROAD
parts.UMÍSTĚNÍ NÁLEPKY PROKAZUJÍCÍ ZAPLACENÍ P: PLACEMENT OF THE STICKER PROVING PAYMENT OF THE FEE FOR THE USE OF MOTORWAYS OR EXPRESSWAYS AND DEFINITION OF SECTIONS TO WHICH THIS FEE DOES NOT APPLY
parts.UZAVÍRKY A OBJÍŽĎKY A ZVLÁŠTNÍ UŽÍVÁNÍ K: ROAD CLOSURES AND DIVERSIONS AND SPECIAL USE OF ROADS

p1.heading: Introductory provisions
p1.text:
(1) For the purposes of this decree, the following shall mean:

- a) „construction of a motorway, road or local road“ (hereinafter referred to as „road“) the permanent or temporary establishment of a new road or its modifications (construction modifications) associated with the reconstruction of the earth body; reconstruction of the earth body means the widening of the road crown, a change in the grade line or relocation of the road axis such that the original external plan or elevation boundaries cannot be maintained,

- b) „road repair“ a modification of a completed structure, in which the external boundaries of the structure are maintained and its parameters are improved and traffic safety is increased,

- c) „road maintenance“ a set of works by which the road is kept in an operationally and technically satisfactory condition under all weather conditions and defects and deficiencies are removed by restoring it to its original state,

- d) „road with unlimited access“ any road other than an expressway,

- e) „immutable parameters“ properties determining the traffic-technical condition of the road that do not change without construction intervention (horizontal and vertical alignment, width arrangement, pavement structure, intersections and structures),

- f) „variable parameters“ properties determining the structural condition of the road, which changes over time due to the effects of weather, traffic load and material aging (roughness, longitudinal and transverse unevenness, pavement defects, bearing capacity, or residual service life),

- g) „pavement management systems“ providing current information on the condition of motorways and roads to the relevant road administrative authorities and administrators of these roads, the purpose of which is to optimize construction activities based on acquired data and knowledge of available technologies with the aim of achieving the best use of invested funds, or achieving other chosen priorities.

(2) A level connection is not an intersection if it is a connection of

- a) a field or forest road,

- b) a service road that is not publicly accessible,

- c) a stop for public transport, a petrol station, a motel, a roadside restaurant, a car park, a rest area, etc.,

- d) an adjacent property

to a road or local road.

(3) A separate deceleration lane (wedge) used for vehicles to exit a road or local road onto an adjacent property is not an auxiliary lane and is not part of the road concerned.

p2.heading: Designation of motorways, roads and local roads
p2.text:
(to § 6 para. 4 and § 9 para. 4 of the Act)

(1) Motorways and roads are designated by numbers by the owner (§ 9 para. 2 of the Act). Motorways and class I roads are designated by numbers from 1 to 99; in the case of a motorway, the letter „D“ is placed before the number in the register, and in the case of an expressway, the letter „R“ is placed before the number. Class II roads are designated by numbers from 101 to 999. Class III roads are designated only in the register by four to five-digit numbers according to the nearest class I or II road, possibly supplemented by a lowercase letter index. Arms of one-way roads are designated by an uppercase letter index.

(2) Roads designated according to a special regulation^1) for international traffic are also designated by the letter „E“ and a number.

(3) Kilometre markers indicate the distance in kilometres from the beginning of the chainage of the motorway or class I road.

(4) The patterns of designation according to paragraphs 1 to 3 are stipulated by a special regulation.^2)

(5) For registration purposes, local roads are designated by Arabic numerals starting with number 1, generally separately for each class of local roads. An alphabetical character is used to designate the class:

- a) for class I local roads, the letter a, e.g. 1a, 2a,

- b) for class II local roads, the letter b, e.g. 1b, 4b,

- c) for class III local roads, the letter c, e.g. 1c, 8c,

- d) for class IV local roads, the letter d, e.g. 1d, 12d.

(6) Bridge and other structures (underpasses, railway crossings, tunnels) on roads are designated by serial numbers starting with number 1 from the beginning of the road chainage. For motorways and roads, the structure number consists of the number of the road concerned and a serial number of the structure separated by a hyphen, possibly supplemented by three indices. The structure number on a local road consists of the local road designation (number and class), a structure symbol separated by a hyphen (e.g. M = bridge) and its serial number. Details of bridge numbering on roads are contained in the binding ČSN 73 6220.

p3.text:
(to § 6 para. 4 of the Act)

(1) Class I local roads are express local roads and the most important collector roads in cities.

(2) Class II local roads are collector roads that connect parts of cities with each other or connect cities, or their parts, to higher class or category land communications.

(3) Class III local roads are service local roads in cities and municipalities enabling direct traffic service to individual buildings, if they are accessible to normal motor vehicle traffic.

(4) Class IV local roads are separate pavements, pedestrian paths, cycle paths, paths in cottage areas, underpasses, footbridges, stairs, footpaths, traffic-calmed roads, residential and pedestrian zones, etc.

p4.heading: Through section of a motorway and road
p4.text:
(to § 8 (3) of the Act)

(1) When determining the boundary of a through section, the relevant building authority^3) shall proceed from the actual state of continuous development along the road; building gaps of up to 200 m do not usually interrupt continuous development.

(2) The placement of a traffic sign^2) indicating a municipality or the end of a municipality does not have to be identical with the boundary of the through section of a motorway or road.

p5.heading: Road Register
p5.text:
(1) The basic road register is the passport, which is maintained by their administrators.

(2) The scope and method of maintaining the passport for motorways and roads shall be determined by the owner (§ 9 (2) of the Act).

(3) The minimum scope of the local road register includes the length of local roads of I to III class in km, the number and total length of bridges on them in km, and the volume of financial resources expended on their construction and separately on their maintenance.

p6.heading: Road Inspections
p6.text:
(1) The inspection is ensured by the owner or administrator of the road concerned, and a record of its result is kept. Inspections are divided into routine, main, and extraordinary.

(2) Routine inspections primarily ascertain the correct function of traffic signs, safety equipment, and defects in road usability (passability) within the following deadlines:

| motorways and expressways | every working day, |
| --- | --- |

| other class I roads | twice a week, |
| --- | --- |

| class II roads | twice a month, |
| --- | --- |
| class III roads | once a month. |

p7.heading: Main and Extraordinary Road Inspection
p7.text:
(1) Unless otherwise stipulated, the main inspection is ensured by the owner or administrator of the road concerned, or in cooperation with a natural or legal person authorized to acquire data for pavement management systems. The purpose of the inspection is to ascertain the structural and technical condition of the road, including its components and accessories.

(2) The main inspection shall be carried out:

- a) upon commissioning of a new or reconstructed road section and before the expiry of the warranty period,

- b) during road inventory.

(3) The scope and method of conducting main inspections and the method of recording them are specified in Annex No. 2.

(4) An extraordinary inspection is ensured by the owner or administrator outside the dates of routine and main inspections, specifically:

- a) in case of sudden damage to the pavement (e.g., by a traffic accident, natural disaster),

- b) in case of a significant change in traffic load (e.g., due to a detour order),

- c) when it is necessary to obtain input data for pavement management systems.

(5) Data obtained from a main or extraordinary inspection may be effectively supplemented by diagnostic measurement of variable parameters and are used in records and pavement management systems.

p8.heading: Bridge Structure Inspections
p8.text:
(1) Bridge inspections are divided into routine, main, control, and extraordinary.

(2) Inspections of bridge structures are ensured by their owner or administrator. The obligation to perform inspections continues even during the temporary exclusion of the bridge from operation or before its re-commissioning.

(3) The scope and method of conducting bridge structure inspections, their intervals, record-keeping, and other details are specified in the recommended ČSN 73 6221.

(4) If a hazardous condition of a bridge is found, measures necessary to ensure traffic safety must be taken without delay, especially closing the bridge and marking a detour, temporary repair of the damaged area, etc.

p9.heading: Road Maintenance and Repairs
p9.text:
(1) The aim of maintenance and repairs is to eliminate defects in road usability, wear, or damage to the road, its components, and accessories. The scope and method of execution depend on the evaluation of inspection results, or on recommendations from the pavement management system.

(2) Maintenance also includes measures that, immediately upon detection of a defect, ensure the regulation of traffic on defective road sections. This particularly includes:

- a) closure of the defective (including snow-covered) section,

- b) marking a detour and placing relevant traffic signs and devices,

- c) immediate provisional ensuring of operational safety (e.g., optical replacement of restraint systems, emergency propping or covering of collapses and landslides, removal of fixed obstacles).

The administrator is obliged to immediately notify the relevant road administrative authority of measures taken according to letters a) and b).

(3) According to the scope and nature of the work, activities under the preceding paragraphs are divided into routine and continuous maintenance and repairs.

(4) Routine maintenance primarily includes minor, locally defined works, including the care of road vegetation.

(5) Continuous maintenance primarily includes more extensive works serving to preserve and restore the original properties of the road pavement by restoring or improving the variable parameters of its wearing course.

(6) Within the scope of repairs, construction works eliminate defects, wear, or damage to the road, its components, and accessories, or improve the quality of the structure and increase operational safety. Repairs lead to the restoration or improvement of all pavement parameters, and possibly also to the improvement of some parameters of other road components and accessories.

(7) The scope and method of road maintenance and repairs are specified in Annex No. 5.

p10.heading: Bridge Maintenance and Repairs
p10.text:
(1) Bridge maintenance and repairs shall be carried out as part of technical care for bridges based on technical documentation, inspection results, diagnostic surveys, or repair documentation. The obligation to perform bridge maintenance and repairs begins on the date of issuance of the occupancy permit and continues even before the bridge is put into operation and during its temporary exclusion from operation.

(2) Bridge maintenance and repairs (including embankment and cut slopes beneath them) shall be ensured by the bridge owner (administrator). Maintenance and repairs of roads, watercourses, reservoirs, other structures or areas beneath the bridge (with the exception of slopes), or third-party equipment on or beneath the bridge shall be ensured by the respective owner (administrator) of such equipment or area, always in agreement with the bridge owner (administrator).

(3) During bridge maintenance and repairs, the area beneath the bridges, especially watercourses and protected water management areas, must not be contaminated.

(4) Bridge maintenance comprises a set of works by which bridges and their equipment are kept in proper technical, safe, and passable condition under all weather and normal traffic conditions. This maintenance shall be carried out continuously throughout the year.

(5) During bridge repair, identified damages or defects shall be removed by construction works carried out according to approved documentation.

(6) More detailed information on bridge maintenance and repairs is contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6221.

p11.heading: Conditions for Interconnecting Land Communications
p11.text:
(1) Interconnections of land communications shall be established in such a way that their location and design comply with road traffic safety, ensure the necessary traffic capacity, adequate visibility, conditions for smooth flow and passage of traffic streams, and proper drainage. The minimum distances of newly constructed intersections on communications, the location and arrangement of intersections are contained in the binding ČSN 73 6102, and further details are provided in the recommended ČSN 73 6101 and ČSN 73 6110.

(2) The determination of the boundary between land communications of a higher and lower category or class in the area of intersections and the connection of access roads is set out in Annex No. 3, with the land communication of greater traffic importance, given by a higher category or class, being considered the main communication.

p12.heading: Conditions for Connecting Adjacent Properties to Roads and Local Communications
p12.text:
(1) Access and egress ramps to a road and local communication may be established only if they meet the following conditions:

- a) visibility for the decision to enter the communication,

- b) visibility for the communication user at least for stopping the vehicle; a vehicle that stops when turning left onto an access ramp must not obstruct the passage of other vehicles in the straight direction, or it must be visible from a distance necessary for stopping another vehicle,

- c) mutual connection distances acceptable from the perspective of traffic safety and fluidity.

More detailed information is contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6101 and ČSN 73 6110.

(2) The width of the access or egress ramp must allow vehicles to turn smoothly off the road or local communication and to enter it. Parameters of turning curves are contained in the binding ČSN 73 6102. If a culvert is part of the access or egress ramp, it must have at least the following nominal pipe clearances:

- a) 400 mm for a culvert length up to 6.00 m,

- b) 600 mm for a culvert length from 6.00 to 10.00 m and for a culvert length over 10.00 m with a culvert slope above 2%,

- c) 800 mm for a culvert length over 10.00 m with a culvert slope up to 2%.

(3) The access and egress ramp shall be established with paving that meets the anticipated traffic load and with an easily cleanable road surface. The structural arrangement must be such as to prevent rainwater from flowing onto the communication and contaminating it.

(4) If the establishment of an access and egress ramp or their alteration, or cancellation, is necessitated by the needs of a natural or legal person who is the owner of the property or has other rights to it, then this person is the „builder“^4) of the connection. In the event that the need for alteration or cancellation of an access or egress ramp arises as a result of construction works on the communication, the builder of the access or egress ramp is the builder of the affected communication.

(5) If a part of an access or egress ramp on the communication's land is being established, structurally modified, or cancelled, the owner of the connected property or a person with other rights to this property must secure prior consent from the owner of the affected communication.

(6) The owner of the access or egress ramp shall ensure proper maintenance of the entire connection, including the culvert.

p13.heading: Conditions for Connecting Other Properties to the Communication
p13.text:
(1) Facilities listed in § 1 para. 2 letter c) shall be connected to roads, excluding expressways, according to local conditions, so as not to reduce traffic fluidity and safety, preferably on their through sections. These connections shall be established with deceleration and acceleration lanes; by decision of the road administrative authority, connection may be permitted only with a deceleration section, without an acceleration section.

(2) The facilities listed in § 1 para. 2 letter c) shall generally be designed to the right on separate one-way lanes or strips, or on areas separated from the continuous clear width by a lateral dividing strip or dividing island, or by horizontal traffic signs. On a two-lane road or local communication with low traffic intensity, the road administrative authority may permit a left-turn at grade, if it does not endanger traffic safety and fluidity.

(3) Technical parameters of acceleration and deceleration lanes, deceleration and acceleration sections, curve radii and widening of the traffic lane in a curve, and visibility conditions are contained in the binding ČSN 73 6102. Further details are contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6101 and ČSN 73 6110. The determination of the connection boundary is set out in Annex No. 3.

p14.heading: Construction Modifications Requiring Notification
p14.text:
(1) The following works are subject to notification to the special building authority, provided they are not listed in § 15, do not affect the rights of third parties, and concern:

- a) minor structures according to a special regulation,^5) as well as portal structures for traffic signs and stockpiles of loose maintenance materials,

- b) building modifications that do not change the appearance of the structure or its method of use, do not interfere with the load-bearing structures of the building, or do not increase the permanent load on a bridge,

- c) maintenance works whose execution could affect the stability of the structure, its appearance, or the environment, and all maintenance works on a structure that is a cultural monument or is located within the territory of a monument reservation or monument zone,

- d) road modifications where the change in the original elevation does not exceed

- 1. 3 cm for roads with directly adjacent buildings or directly adjacent sidewalks, at railway crossings, on bridges, in underpasses, and in places where utility networks and other lines are located,

- 2. 10 cm for roads not listed in the preceding point.

(2) Provided the conditions stipulated in paragraph 1 are met, these works include, in particular:

- a) modifications to roadways and verges beyond the scope of routine maintenance listed in Annex No. 5,

- b) establishment of curbs for the roadway,

- c) local widening of narrowed sections of the road crown and roadway in open country,

- d) raising or strengthening of sidewalks with height adjustment of curbs,

- e) remediation works for landslides or in undermined areas,

- f) renewal of culverts and walls,

- g) establishment of drainage chutes on road embankment slopes, upslope drainage ditches or trenches,

- h) establishment of additional crossovers of the central dividing strip,

- i) supplementing existing or constructing new road lighting,

- j) widening of paved areas at existing rest stops,

- k) repair of bridge expansion joints or insulation systems on bridges.

p15.heading: Building modifications and activities that do not require notification
p15.text:
(1) Neither a building permit nor notification to the special building authority is required for works carried out on roads as part of their maintenance, if

- a) their execution cannot affect the stability of the structure (by interfering with load-bearing structures), its appearance, or the environment,

- b) they are not carried out on a structure that is a cultural monument or is located within the territory of a monument reservation or monument zone,

- c) there is no widening of the road, change in its route, or elevation,

- d) legally protected interests and rights of other persons, including rights arising from the intersection of the road with other transport routes, utility networks, other lines, waters and water management works, and protected areas (§ 36 and 37 of the Act), cannot be affected,

- e) they do not require a partial or complete road closure (§ 24 of the Act).

(2) Works according to paragraph 1 are specified in more detail in Annexes No. 5 and 7.

p16.heading: Preparation of constructions, construction and building modifications
p16.text: The preparation of constructions, construction of roads and their building modifications shall proceed in accordance with special regulations^6) and binding Czech technical standards listed in Annex No. 1 under Nos. 1 to 29. Further details are contained in the recommended Czech technical standards listed in Annex No. 1 under Nos. 30 to 66. When making building modifications to existing roads, it is necessary, according to local conditions, to improve the quality of the road by increasing safety and smoothness of traffic on it, or at least to contribute to the homogeneity of the entire stretch.

p17.heading: Design elements and categories
p17.text:
(1) The design elements of the road must be chosen to provide all users with appropriate conditions for smooth and safe driving, with regard to the required function of the road and taking into account the tolerable load on the area.^7)

(2) The design elements and arrangement of roads depend on the choice of the road's design category, which is selected according to the projected traffic intensity, characteristics of the area, or development, and general technical and economic contexts.

(3) Each design category of a road is defined by the width of the road and the design speed. The design speed is such a speed at which individual vehicles can safely travel on this road. More detailed information on the range of design speeds for individual road design categories is contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6101 and ČSN 73 6110.

p18.heading: Horizontal and vertical alignment
p18.text:
(1) The horizontal and vertical alignment of the road route must be mutually harmonized and adapted to the traffic significance and category of the road, as well as to the safety and smoothness of traffic on it.

(2) The smallest design values of horizontal and vertical elements shall be proposed only in justified cases and must not be accumulated or used at or near intersections or at other locations critical from the perspective of traffic safety.

(3) Sight distances must be equal to the distance required to stop before an obstacle on the roadway along the entire newly constructed road route.

(4) On roads with a single two-way carriageway, sections with a greater than minimum sight distance for overtaking should account for the largest possible proportion of the total road length and be distributed as evenly as possible along it.

(5) Details on the smallest design values of horizontal and vertical elements, the largest longitudinal and resultant gradients, and the smallest sight distances for stopping and overtaking are contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6101 and ČSN 73 6110.

p19.heading: Cross-sectional arrangement on sections between intersections
p19.text:
(1) The road crown includes traffic lanes, auxiliary and associated lanes, guide strips, shoulders, possibly a central or even a side median strip, sidewalks and strips or lanes for pedestrians and cyclists. Public sidewalks and strips or lanes for pedestrians and cyclists are not permissible within the crown of motorways and expressways.

(2) Auxiliary lanes for slow vehicles or an increased number of traffic lanes are established primarily on uphill sections if the proportion of slow vehicles in the traffic flow and their driving speeds cause an unacceptable reduction in traffic fluidity.

(3) Bus stop lanes for scheduled passenger transport are connected to roads, excluding expressways, and to local roads in accordance with the binding ČSN 73 6425.

(4) The unpaved part of the shoulder must be wide enough to facilitate visibility and provide sufficient space for the placement of road components and accessories where necessary.

(5) The width of the central median strip on very confined sections may be reduced, but the width necessary for the installation of a safety barrier must be maintained. At the same time, stopping sight distance in a horizontal curve must be ensured. The central median strip on motorways and expressways must be equipped with a safety barrier; on other directionally divided roads, a safety barrier is installed depending on traffic intensity and composition, permitted driving speed, and the width of the median strip.

(6) The basic cross slope of the carriageway on newly constructed sections must be at least 2.5%, and on reconstructed sections at least 2%. More detailed information on sections where the two-way cross slope changes to a one-way cross slope, and on the provisions of paragraphs 2 to 5, is contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6101 and ČSN 73 6110.

p20.heading: Intersections
p20.text:
(1) The shape of an intersection is chosen according to the intensity of intersecting traffic flows, the category of intersecting roads, and the location of the intersection in the terrain. The type and design elements of an intersection are contained in the binding ČSN 73 6102.

(2) When designing at-grade intersections, the best possible visibility conditions must be ensured, and the geometry of the intersection is resolved as simply as possible in accordance with its function, so that it is clear and understandable to users and so that the main and secondary directions are highlighted in terms of right-of-way.

p21.heading: Road body
p21.text: The design of the road pavement and subgrade, their drainage and protection, and the type and quality of materials used are based on an evaluation of the traffic significance, traffic load of the road, type of subsoil, and climatic conditions, in accordance with the binding Czech technical standards listed in Annex No. 1 under Nos. 5 to 16 and 23. Details on the road subgrade are contained in the recommended Czech technical standards listed in Annex No. 1 under Nos. 36 to 40, 44, 49 and 52.

p22.heading: Environment and integration into the landscape
p22.text:
(1) The preparation and execution of road construction and modifications shall be based on the requirements for nature and environmental protection in accordance with special regulations.^8)

(2) The integration of the road alignment into the landscape and the planting of greenery shall be addressed sensitively according to local conditions, also with regard to protection against glare and adverse weather conditions (wind, snowfall, etc.). Noise barriers along the road must be constructed in such a way as to be integrated into the surrounding landscape as gently as possible, and shall be established only in cases where it is not possible to reduce the harmful effects of traffic noise by other means.

p23.heading: Components and accessories
p23.text: Technical and functional requirements for traffic light signaling devices are contained in the binding ČSN 36 5601 and ČSN 73 6021. Details on the technical execution and spatial arrangement of other road components and accessories are provided in the recommended ČSN 73 6101 and ČSN 73 6110.

p24.heading: Traffic signs, traffic and safety devices
p24.text:
(1) Vertical and horizontal traffic signs must be designed, executed, and placed in accordance with a special regulation.^2) Further details are contained in the recommended ČSN 01 8020.

(2) Artificial screens or hedges shall be established in the central median strip of motorway and expressway sections, or between parallel roads.

(3) Escape ramps shall be established to ensure the safety of heavy goods vehicle traffic on exceptionally steep or long descents. Speed bumps and raised pedestrian crossings enforce compliance with the prescribed permitted speed. Before a speed bump, appropriate traffic signs must be installed in accordance with a special regulation.^2) Further details are provided in the recommended ČSN 73 6101 and ČSN 73 6110.

(4) On motorways and expressways, specially marked emergency telephone posts or other communication means (call boxes) connected to a continuously operating dispatch center shall be installed. Call boxes shall be installed on the outer part of the shoulder at a distance from structures, typically every 2 km, and secured by the installation of guardrails.

p25.heading: Public lighting
p25.text: Motorways and roads shall always be illuminated in built-up areas of municipalities. Outside these areas, only specially designated sections shall be illuminated, such as at border crossings, in tunnels and their adjacent sections, exceptionally at intersections, under the conditions contained in the binding ČSN 73 6102 and ČSN 73 7507. Lighting may also be established in areas where traffic intensity, or the frequency of pedestrians and cyclists, justifies it. Details are contained in the recommended Czech technical standards listed in Annex No. 1 under Nos. 33, 34, 35, 49 and 51.

p26.heading: Rest areas
p26.text:
(1) Rest areas shall be constructed along motorways and expressways, according to the nature of traffic, situated in areas outside intersections and at mutual distances of at least 25 km. The basic equipment of a rest area must include parking spaces for 25 passenger vehicles, ten lorries, four buses, sanitary facilities with adequate capacity and continuous year-round operation, a water and electricity source, rest areas with tables, benches, and waste bins.

(2) All newly constructed operational and parking areas of a rest area must be physically separated from the carriageway (carriageways) of the motorway or expressway and must also allow their use by citizens dependent on a wheelchair.

(3) More detailed information on newly constructed rest areas along other roads is contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6101.

p27.heading: Clearance and Passage Space
p27.text:
(1) The shape and dimensions of the clearance and passage space shall be determined according to the width arrangement, category, and class of the road by the binding ČSN 73 6201; with the exception of cases specified therein, no parts of structures and facilities may encroach upon this space.

(2) The clear height on bridge structures and underpasses shall be determined based on the shape and dimensions of the clearance and passage space. In underpasses, the binding ČSN 73 6201 specifies the safety distance between the upper outline of the clearance space and the lower edge of the load-bearing structure, as well as the safety distance from any overhead contact line.

(3) The minimum clear height above the surface of the carriageway and pavements shall also be determined according to the provisions of paragraph 2.

p28.heading: Width Arrangement on Bridge Structures
p28.text:
(1) The width and number of carriageways, or traffic lanes, auxiliary and associated lanes, guide strips, verges, pavements, and the central median strip on a bridge structure or in an underpass shall be determined essentially in the same way as on adjacent sections of the road. With the consent of the road administrative authority, it is permitted in justified cases to propose a reduction in the width of verges on bridges longer than 100 m, on directionally separated roads with excluded pedestrian and cyclist traffic, and also a reduction in the width of emergency lanes. Parking, stopping, and bus stop lanes are not designed on bridge structures and in underpasses. The unpaved part of the verge shall be paved on a bridge structure without backfill.

(2) Pedestrian or cyclist paths on bridge structures generally run on separate (raised) pavements. The width of a public pavement includes the width of the passage space, or the width of the crash barrier, supports for supplementary facilities, and the width of the raised kerb, which extends no more than 0.50 m into the clearance space of the road. Emergency pavements are designed according to the binding ČSN 73 6201.

(3) Permanent footbridges for pedestrians and permanent underpasses must have a clear width of at least 2.00 m.

p29.heading: Restraint Safety and Permanent Devices
p29.text:
(1) The required containment level and requirements for the placement and construction of crash barriers and parapet crash barriers are contained in the binding ČSN 73 6201.

(2) Crash barriers must also be placed where protection of supports in a road underpass is necessary or to ensure road traffic safety against impact with a fixed obstacle.

(3) On bridges with a permitted speed of no more than 60 km/h, pavements can be separated only by reflective kerbs with a height of 0.12 m to 0.20 m.

(4) On the outer side of pavements, footbridges for pedestrians and cyclists, inspection walkways, and cornices of backfilled structures at a height greater than 2.00 m, railings shall be installed according to the binding ČSN 73 6201.

(5) Special regulations apply to permanent devices on bridges.^9)

p30.heading: Loading of Bridge Structures
p30.text:
(1) Bridge structures are designed for loads, which are divided into two classes:

- a) load class A - for all bridges carrying motorways, class I and II roads, furthermore class III roads designated by the relevant road administrative authority, and class I and II local roads,

- b) load class B - for class III local roads and class III roads not designated as class A according to the provisions of letter a).

Further details are contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6203.

(2) Exceptional loading of bridges by special vehicles or combinations, which are permitted for road traffic only with a permit, shall be assessed on the basis of a static calculation as a special use of the road according to § 25 of the Act.

p31.heading: Bridge Load Capacity
p31.text:
(1) The determination of bridge load capacity, and its marking on bridges if applicable, is ensured by the owner or administrator of the bridge.

(2) For all existing bridges on roads, load capacities shall be determined according to the binding ČSN 73 6220; in case of lower load capacity (normal, exceptional, or by a single axle), appropriate traffic signs must be installed.^2)

p32.heading: Tunnels
p32.text:
(1) The spatial arrangement of tunnels on roads must comply with the clearance gauge determined according to further provisions. No parts of structures, structural elements, and equipment (lighting, ventilation, signaling, traffic signs, drainage, electrical equipment, etc.) may encroach upon this gauge, with the exception of restraint safety devices.

(2) The minimum width of the carriageway between kerbs for a single carriageway in a tunnel tube is 7.50 m, with the minimum width of traffic lanes in the tunnel being 3.50 m and guide strips 0.25 m. The minimum width of service pavements is 0.75 m. The height of the tunnel clearance gauge is generally 4.50 m.

(3) Detailed conditions for the transverse arrangement of the tunnel, safety structural modifications (emergency and turning bays, escape routes), and tunnel equipment (lighting, ventilation, safety devices, traffic control) are contained in the binding ČSN 73 7507, depending on the tunnel length, road category, design speed, intensity, and organization of road traffic.

p33.heading: Galleries and Similar Facilities
p33.text:
(1) In places where traffic on communications may be endangered by falling rocks, other foreign objects, and avalanches, shelters (galleries) or similar protective structures, such as protective nets, retaining walls, and other devices, shall be established in the cut, in accordance with the binding ČSN 73 6201; further details are contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6101.

(2) Requirements for clearance and pedestrian space, width and height arrangement, and construction guidelines (e.g., protection of articulated supports) are the same as for underpasses (§ 27).

p34.heading: Walls
p34.text:
(1) Retaining and abutment walls that directly ensure the stability of the communication body shall be designed in accordance with the binding ČSN 73 6201.

(2) The width arrangement of the clearance space above unbackfilled retaining walls higher than 2.00 m and the principles for designing safety restraint systems are specified in the binding ČSN 73 6201.

(3) Information regarding paragraph 1 and the width arrangement of the clearance space above unbackfilled retaining walls with a height less than or equal to 2.00 m, above backfilled retaining walls regardless of their height, and along abutment walls is contained in the recommended Czech technical standards listed in Annex No. 1 under Nos. 40, 49, 52, 57.

p35.heading: Culverts
p35.text: A culvert in the communication body is a structure that conveys surface water with any cross-sectional shape and with a clear vertical opening of up to and including 2.00 m. The dimensions of culvert openings shall be determined by hydraulic calculation, with the smallest opening dimension being 600 mm. Details are regulated by the binding ČSN 73 6201.

p36.heading: Noise barriers and embankments
p36.text:
(1) Noise barriers along the communication must be located outside the clearance and pedestrian space, depending on their resistance to vehicle impact and deformation depth, or the displacement of guardrails. In a wall longer than 300 m, escape openings shall be established at a distance of no more than 150 m. Details on the placement of noise barriers are contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6101 and ČSN 73 6110.

(2) If a noise barrier is placed on the outer edge of a sidewalk on a bridge, the width of the pedestrian space must always be maintained in accordance with the binding ČSN 73 6201.

(3) Earth embankments shall be planted with vegetation or provided with other erosion protection. A wall combined with an earth embankment lower than 1.50 m shall be protected by a guardrail.

p37.text: The sticker must be placed and its entire surface directly affixed to the inner side of the front window of the motor vehicle (or the first motor vehicle in the set) on its lower or upper right edge, so that the driver's view from the vehicle is restricted as little as possible and the sticker is clearly visible from the outer front side of the vehicle.

p38.text:
(1) Sections of motorways and expressways designated by a traffic sign^2) as a motorway or as a road for motor vehicles, for the use of which no fee is payable, are listed in Annex No. 4. Similarly, express local communications must also be designated.

(2) On the sections of communications listed in paragraph 1, the traffic sign shall be supplemented by an additional panel in accordance with a special regulation^2) with the inscription „Bez poplatku“ (No fee).

p39.heading: Closures and detours
p39.text:
(to § 24 para. 9 of the Act)

(1) If more than half of one traffic lane is closed for a length greater than 50 m and for a period longer than three days, the application for permission for the closure and ordering of a detour shall be submitted in duplicate to the relevant road administrative authority by the person in whose interest or due to whose activity the closure is to be permitted and the detour ordered; if the reason is construction work, the application shall be submitted by the contractor, unless the relevant road administrative authority stipulates otherwise.

(2) The application for permission for the closure and ordering of a detour of a communication shall contain:

- a) precise identification of the closure according to the distance marking in km and m from the beginning of the affected communication (hereinafter referred to as „chainage“), or the topographical course,

- b) the duration of the closure with a possible option of its interruption on non-working days and public holidays,

- c) the reason for the closure (if the reason is the performance of construction work, also its scope, method of performance, and identification of who is to perform this work),

- d) a proposal for the detour route including a graphic annex,

- e) the name and surname of the employee responsible for organizing and securing the event that is the reason for submitting the application, the address and telephone number of his workplace and residence,

- f) if the requested duration of the closure and detour is longer than three days and concerns construction work, a work schedule containing the quantity and time course of individual types of work must be provided,

- g) the consent of the affected transport authority, if the closure requires the temporary relocation of scheduled public transport stops.

(3) Unless it concerns accidents (§ 24 para. 8 of the Act), the application for permission for the closure shall be delivered to the relevant road administrative authority no later than 30 days before the requested day of the communication closure.

(4) The marking of a closure or detour is regulated by a special regulation.^2) In the cases listed in paragraph 2 letter f), an orientation board must also be placed at the beginning of the closure, indicating the start and end dates of the closure, the name and registered office of the legal entity, or the name and residence of the natural person, at whose request the closure was permitted.

(5) The decision on permitting the closure and ordering the detour shall contain:

- a) precise identification of the closure according to the chainage of the affected communication, or the topographical course,

- b) the duration of the closure,

- c) the determination of the detour route,

- d) the name and surname of the employee responsible for organizing and securing the event (work),

- e) the location of the scheduled public transport stop, if the closure requires its temporary relocation,

- f) the determination of traffic signs by the relevant authority of the Police of the Czech Republic at the place of closure and on the detour route.

p40.heading: Special use of communications
p40.text:
(to § 25 (7) of the Act)

(1) An application for a permit for special use of a road shall be submitted to the road administrative authority by the person in whose interest or due to whose activity the special use of the road is to be permitted; if such a reason is construction work, the application shall be submitted by the contractor, unless the competent road administrative authority stipulates otherwise.

(2) An application for a special use permit pursuant to § 25 (4) (a) of the Act shall contain:

- a) the purpose, scope and duration of the transport, whether and when it will be repeated,

- b) a proposed transport route with a precise indication of the route's course and an approximate indication of the transport's timetable,

- c) the type, model and registration plates of the vehicles to be used for the transport,

- d) the vehicle's weight, the number, load and wheelbase of individual axles, the number, dimension, inflation and type of tires of individual axles, the smallest turning radius of the vehicle or combination and the corresponding smallest outer turning radius,

- e) a drawing of the outline of the vehicle or combination with dimensions and load placement indicated.

(3) Transport operations pursuant to the preceding paragraph with a total weight exceeding 60 tons or of excessive dimensions^10) may be permitted only exceptionally, if the applicant proves that it is not technically feasible to reduce the weight or dimensions of the transport or to use another method of transport, and that the load-bearing capacity of bridges and the bearing capacity of road surfaces, verified by a static assessment, will allow the transport to be carried out.

(4) An application for a special use permit pursuant to § 25 (4) (b) and (f) of the Act shall contain the particulars specified in paragraph 2 (a) to (c) and in paragraph 5 (d); if it concerns special use pursuant to § 25 (4) (f) of the Act, the application shall also contain a proposal for the method of protecting the road surface of the affected road or local road from damage.

(5) An application for a special use permit pursuant to § 25 (4) (c) to (e) of the Act shall contain:

- a) a precise determination of the place, purpose and duration of the special use and the method of its implementation (what type of construction work, what method of sale and in what facility, what scope of the event, etc.),

- b) the name and surname of the person responsible for the course of the special use, their birth number and address (for legal entities, their name, registered office and ID number),

- c) the estimated impact of the special use (or the traffic generated by this use) on the safety and fluidity of traffic on the affected road section and a proposal for resolving the resulting situation,

- d) a permit to operate the subject of activity within the scope of the special use, if such a permit is required by special regulations,^11)

- e) the opinion of the affected administrative authorities, if required by special regulations.^12)

(6) A special use permit pursuant to § 25 (4) (a) of the Act shall contain the route, method and duration of transport; it may also contain, in particular, driving speed, escort and other measures to ensure the safety and fluidity of traffic, the protection of other road users, road surfaces, bridges and railway facilities (crossings, tracks, overhead lines), lines and other utility networks, owners of adjacent real estate, etc.

(7) The permit pursuant to paragraph 6 may further oblige the applicant to notify all affected administrative authorities and real estate owners in writing of the execution of necessary construction and other modifications. In the notification, the applicant shall state the time of execution (or removal), place, purpose, scope and a simple technical description of these modifications. If the necessary modifications concern railways, utility networks and other lines, watercourses, water management and other structures and protected areas, the procedure shall be in accordance with special regulations.^12)

(8) A special use permit pursuant to § 25 (4) (b) and (f) of the Act shall contain the same particulars as the application for this permit pursuant to paragraph 4.

(9) A permit for methods of special use pursuant to § 25 (4) (c) to (e) of the Act shall contain:

- a) a precise determination of the place, purpose and duration of the special use and the method of its implementation (what type of construction work, what method of sale and in what facility, what scope of the event, etc.),

- b) the name and surname of the person responsible for the course of the special use, their birth number and address (for legal entities, their business name, registered office and ID number),

- c) the determination of traffic signs, if their need or change is caused by the special use,

- d) the deadline and method for restoring the road to its original condition (cleaning, tidying up, etc.).

(10) If the special use is to be extended in time or place beyond an already issued permit, a new application must be submitted, the content of which shall be governed mutatis mutandis by the particulars listed in paragraphs 2, 4 and 5, provided that it contains the requested changes and their justification.

p41.heading: Basic Provisions
p41.text:
(1) Winter maintenance, in order of importance, mitigates defects in road passability and local road and through-road section accessibility arising from weather influences and conditions during winter situations.

(2) Winter maintenance shall be carried out according to the winter maintenance plan. In a typical winter situation, the owner (administrator) of the road shall remove or at least mitigate defects in the passability (accessibility) of the road within the time limits stipulated by the winter maintenance plan, the model of which is provided in Annex No. 6.

(3) Measures before the commencement of winter maintenance and an overview of technologies used in winter maintenance are detailed in Annex No. 7; the model of the winter maintenance logbook and the method of its evaluation are provided in Annex No. 8.

(4) For the purposes of this decree, the winter period is the time from November 1st to March 31st of the following year. During this period, winter maintenance shall be carried out according to the winter maintenance plan. If a winter weather situation arises outside this period, defects in road passability (accessibility) shall be mitigated without undue delay, commensurate with the situation that has arisen.

p42.heading: Winter Maintenance Plan
p42.text:
(1) For the purposes of the winter maintenance plan, roads are divided according to their order of importance as follows:

- a) 1st order - class I roads and transport-important class II roads,

- b) 2nd order - remaining sections of class II roads not included in the 1st order and transport-significant class III roads,

- c) 3rd order - other class III roads not included in the 2nd order and generally maintained only by ploughing,

- d) unmaintained - roads on which scheduled public passenger transport is not operated and on which winter maintenance is not necessary due to their negligible transport significance [users must be notified of this fact in the manner stipulated by a special regulation^2)].

(2) Decisions on the preparation of a winter maintenance plan for local roads are made by municipalities according to the size of the municipality and the transport significance of the local roads. If municipalities decide to prepare a plan for ensuring the passability of class I to III local roads, they shall take into account the following order of importance:

- a) 1st order - express and collector local roads with mass public transport and scheduled passenger transport, access local roads to healthcare facilities and other significant local roads,

- b) 2nd order - collector local roads not included in the 1st order and important service local roads,

- c) 3rd order - other service local roads,

- d) unmaintained - local roads on which winter maintenance is not necessary due to their transport insignificance (the municipality shall notify users of this fact in the customary local manner).

p43.heading: Motorways and expressways
p43.text:
(1) Defects in passability are mitigated on all through traffic lanes, then progressively on intersection lanes, slow vehicle lanes, hardened shoulders, on access and exit ramps from rest areas and on rest areas. Thereafter, defects in the walkability of rest areas are mitigated, always during daylight hours.

(2) Under adverse weather conditions, defects in passability are first mitigated on at least one traffic lane in each direction of travel.

(3) Spreading or spraying is carried out primarily with chemical de-icing materials, mainly by means of mechanisms or devices that allow for precise dosing and even distribution of chemical de-icing substances on the road surface. Abrasive materials are used only if the use of chemical de-icing materials is prohibited for the given section, or if their use would not mitigate defects in passability due to the weather situation.

p44.heading: Other roads
p44.text:
During winter maintenance, in accordance with the approved winter maintenance plan, such available technology shall be used that best suits local conditions and the order of importance of the road (§ 42 (1)):

- a) 1st order - the entire width and length of the road surface is maintained

- 1. black ice and residual snow layer after ploughing with a thickness of less than 3 cm are removed by spreading chemical de-icing materials,

- 2. black ice and slipperiness of the snow layer, when chemical de-icing materials are ineffective, are roughened by spreading abrasive materials,

- b) 2nd order - technologies identical to those in the 1st order, with the proviso that, if necessary, compacted snow layers are left on the roads and roughened by spreading abrasive materials. Spreading may only be carried out in places where the traffic-technical condition of the road requires it (intersections, steep ascents, sharp bends, scheduled public passenger transport stops),

- c) 3rd order - maintained after the treatment of 1st and 2nd order roads, essentially by ploughing, and in places where the traffic-technical condition of the road requires it, abrasive materials are spread.

p45.heading: Deadlines for mitigating defects in the passability of motorways and roads
p45.text:
(1) Road administrators ensure winter maintenance so that the instruction to commence the relevant intervention is issued immediately upon ascertaining its necessity and that ploughing is carried out already during snowfall and, if necessary, after its cessation.

(2) The time from the detection of a defect in the passability of a motorway or road to the departure of the first mechanisms to mitigate this defect must not exceed 30 minutes during the winter period. Outside the winter period, defects in passability are mitigated without delay.

(3) Passability must be ensured by spreading operations within the following time limits from the departure of spreading mechanisms:

| a) | on motorways and expressways | within 2 hours, |
| --- | --- | --- |

| b) on roads classified |  |
| --- | --- |
| as 1st order | within 3 hours, |
| 2nd order | within 6 hours, |
| 3rd order | within 12 hours. |

(4) The deadlines specified in paragraph 3 apply to motorways and roads classified as 1st order for the entire 24 hours, and to roads classified as 2nd and 3rd order for the period stipulated in the winter maintenance plan.

p46.heading: Deadlines for mitigating defects in the passability of local roads
p46.text:
Municipalities ensure the passability of class I to III local roads within the following deadlines:

| a) | 1st order of importance | within 4 hours, |
| --- | --- | --- |
| b) | 2nd order of importance | within 12 hours, |
| c) | 3rd order of importance | after treatment of 1st and 2nd order roads, but no later than 48 hours. |

p47.heading: Ensuring passability by cleaning roads and bridges
p47.text:
(1) Road cleaning shall be carried out in the following cases and by the following deadlines:

- a) after the winter period on motorways, class I roads and class I local roads no later than 30 April, on class II and III roads and on class II and III local roads no later than 31 May (removal of abrasive material residues, cleaning of traffic signs and equipment, etc.),

- b) by 30 November, removal of fallen leaves and ensuring drainage functionality,

- c) before commencing continuous maintenance or repair works,

- d) immediately upon detection of extraordinary contamination, especially after accidents and vehicle breakdowns which resulted in a reduction of the anti-skid properties of the road surface (spilled oil and fuels), or in the event of environmental damage risk, provided that the contamination was not immediately removed by the person obliged to do so pursuant to § 28 (1) of the Act,

- e) where possible, during the winter period, removal of excess abrasive material.

(2) The motorway or road administrator shall ensure the cleaning of their through sections to the same extent and within the same deadlines as on adjacent sections of the motorway or road outside built-up areas.

(3) More detailed information on bridge cleaning is contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6221.

p48.heading: Crossings with Watercourses
p48.text:
(1) For new bridge structures over watercourses, a clear height of at least 0.50 m above the design flow level must be maintained in the bridge opening. Design flows, spatial arrangement of bridge openings over other water obstacles, assessment of backwater effects and cases where bridging a watercourse with a bridge structure designed for inlet submergence is permitted, as well as other conditions for crossings with watercourses, are contained in the binding ČSN 73 6201.

(2) If a road crosses a waterway of significant transport importance within the meaning of a special regulation,^13) the bridge opening must meet the conditions stipulated by the special regulation.^14)

p49.heading: Crossings with Railways
p49.text:
(1) On bridge structures over electrified railway, tram, or trolleybus lines, barriers (protective shields and nets, etc.) must be installed to prevent contact with live parts of the traction line. In the case of lines with motor traction, protection against smoke gases must be installed. The conditions for these barriers and protections are contained in the binding ČSN 73 6201 and ČSN 73 6223.

(2) The establishment and method of securing level crossings of roads and local roads with railways in cases of minor transport importance are stipulated by special regulations^2)^,^15) and the binding ČSN 73 6380. More detailed information is also contained in the recommended ČSN 73 6101 and ČSN 73 6110.

p50.heading: Utilities in Road Bodies
p50.text:
(1) When underground utility networks cross a road or local road, tunneling and pipe jacking must be used to a technically and economically feasible extent. Trenching must not be permitted on motorways, expressways, and express local roads outside built-up areas of municipalities. When a larger number of utility networks are laid concurrently within built-up areas of municipalities, these networks must be preferentially laid in combined routes according to the binding ČSN 73 7505.

(2) Above-ground or underground networks for the transport of flammable liquids, liquefied hydrocarbon gases, or other flammable and/or explosive media must not be placed in roads outside built-up areas of municipalities and in tunnels.

(3) The minimum permissible cover for underground utility networks is contained in the binding ČSN 73 6005. More detailed information on the method and spatial arrangement of the interface between roads and utility networks and other lines is contained in the recommended Czech technical standards listed in Annex No. 1 under Nos. 49, 51, 65, and 66.

p51.heading: Utilities on Bridge Structures
p51.text:
(1) The conditions for the routing of third-party equipment on a bridge structure, when crossing it or running parallel to it, are regulated by the binding ČSN 73 6201.

(2) A gas pipeline, product pipelines, or other third-party equipment must not be routed on a bridge structure, in its openings, or within the protective zone of the affected utility, if its position, operation, and sudden failures could cause destruction or damage to the bridge structure, hinder its maintenance, or endanger operational safety on the bridge.

p52.heading: Identification of a Person Authorized to Perform State Supervision
p52.text:
(to § 41 (4) of the Act)

The specimen of the identification card for a person authorized to perform state supervision over land communications is provided in Annex No. 9.

p53.heading: Effectiveness
p53.text:
This Decree shall enter into force on the date of its promulgation.

**Minister:**

**Ing. Říman, m.p.**

sprilohac2kvyhlas.text:
MAIN INSPECTIONS

The aim is to establish a uniform method for recording phenomena observed during main inspections. It also includes establishing criteria for assessing the facts ascertained.

- 1. PRINCIPLES FOR CONDUCTING MAIN INSPECTIONS

- 1.1. Main inspections are carried out using a nodal localization system; for motorways, a linear localization system is also permissible.

In the case of local roads, the data collection and subsequent processing system shall be adapted to the localization system used.

- 1.2. The entire road body, including its components and accessories, is monitored. In the case of a directionally divided four-lane road, each carriageway is considered a separate road.

- 1.3. Main bridge inspections are carried out according to the recommended ČSN 73 6221.

- 2. METHOD OF RECORDING OBSERVED PHENOMENA

- 2.1. Road surface and road body (including shoulder)

For defects, their extent (area, length) and location on the road surface are monitored.

Phenomena identified during the main inspection are supplemented with available data on variable parameters (roughness, longitudinal and transverse unevenness, residual service life), measured and evaluated on the basis of the relevant ČSN standards listed in Annex No. 1**.**

- 2.2. Bridge structures are monitored according to the binding ČSN 73 6220 and the recommended ČSN 73 6221.

- 2.3. Road embankment, drainage facilities and other structures excluding bridges.

Their occurrence, location (position and length), structural-technical condition, and functionality are recorded.

Classification of structural-technical condition

I. excellent

without apparent defects

II. good

minor defects not affecting functionality and not immediately affecting service life

III. satisfactory

more serious defects having a partial impact on functionality and, without maintenance or repair, also on service life

IV. unsatisfactory

serious defects, almost preventing functionality, service life is minimal

V. emergency

the element is non-functional, service life is zero

- 2.4. Other components and accessories of the road

Their occurrence, location (position, or length) and technical condition are recorded. The actual condition is checked against the data in the asset register. Increased attention is paid to the condition of traffic signs and traffic equipment.

- 3. EVALUATION OF IDENTIFIED PHENOMENA

- 3.1. Road surface (including shoulder)

Evaluation of identified phenomena is typically carried out within road management systems.

The design level of damage is determined depending on the traffic load of the road and its traffic importance.

Measurement and evaluation are carried out by approved types of measuring instruments in accordance with the recommended ČSN standards listed under Nos. 53, 54 and 55 in Annex No. 1. In justified cases, measurements must be supplemented by probes into the road structure and laboratory tests of embedded materials.

When two or more defects occur simultaneously, the most serious defect is always given priority.

Based on the ascertained values of operational serviceability characteristics or the extent of individual road surface defects, the road surface is divided into homogeneous sections and classified into five classification grades, and a decision is made on the method of maintenance or repair.

Maintenance (both routine and continuous) or repair can usually be proposed with multiple technology variants. Their selection primarily depends on economic assessment.

- 3.2. Bridge structures

Evaluation of identified phenomena and proposal of measures are carried out according to the binding ČSN 73 6220 and the recommended ČSN 73 6221.

- 3.3. Road embankment, drainage facilities and other structures excluding bridges

Proposed measures correspond to the classification of the structural condition according to point 2.3.

I. excellent - no measures

II. satisfactory - routine maintenance according to plan

III. good - routine or continuous maintenance, inclusion in the repair plan

IV. unsatisfactory - execution of repair or continuous maintenance

V. emergency - immediate execution of repair, reconstruction, traffic-organizational measures

Measures, with the exception of emergency status, are incorporated into the work schedule for the next period.

- 3.4. Other components and accessories of the road

Based on the ascertained facts, the road administrator's work schedule is refined.

sprilohac5kvyhlas.text:
MAINTENANCE AND REPAIR OF ROADS

- 1. ROUTINE MAINTENANCE OF ROADS

Routine maintenance includes minor, locally defined works, the need for which was identified during road inspections.

This primarily involves the following works:

- 1.1. maintenance of carriageway and verges

- repair of asphalt pavements

- repair of cement concrete pavements

- levelling and maintenance of paved surfaces

- maintenance of gravel surfaces

- trimming, supplementing, strengthening and cleaning of verges

- 1.2. maintenance of traffic signs, traffic devices and other accessories

(vertical and horizontal traffic signs and devices, mirrors, call boxes, public lighting, traffic light signalling devices, railings, reflectors, crash barriers, flexible barriers, guide posts, snow fences, storage facilities and dumps for maintenance materials, etc.)

- 1.3. maintenance of drainage facilities

(culverts, ditches, channels, chutes, soakaways and infiltration pits, road sewerage, street inlets and silt traps)

- 1.4. maintenance of slopes and embankments of the road earthworks

- 1.5. maintenance of pavements and other non-motorised roads, median strips and traffic islands

- 1.6. maintenance of areas and equipment of rest areas, lay-bys and parking areas and other road components

(kilometre markers, boundary stones, speed bumps, escape lanes, noise barriers and embankments)

- 1.7. maintenance of structures

(tunnels, galleries, retaining, revetment, cladding and parapet walls, terraces)

- 1.8. care of road vegetation

- 2. CONTINUOUS MAINTENANCE OF ROADS

Continuous maintenance includes more extensive works in continuous sections serving to preserve and restore original properties. The basis for the decision on its execution are the results of pavement management systems, or evaluated data from road inspections.

This primarily involves the following works:

- 2.1. renewal of worn wearing course, strengthening and adjustment of verges, pavements and other non-motorised roads

- carriageways with asphalt pavement

pavement renewal

renewal of anti-skid properties of pavement

pavement evenness renewal

methods using recycling of original pavement

- carriageways with cement concrete pavement

renewal of anti-skid properties of pavement

pavement evenness renewal

- carriageways with paved surface or with road slab surface

renewal of anti-skid properties and evenness of pavement

strengthening

- gravel roads

pavement evenness renewal

strengthening

- 2.2. renewal of individual types of road components and accessories (in a continuous stretch)

- 2.3. adjustment of earthworks and its securing by constructing walls

- 2.4. removal of frost heaves in continuous sections

- 2.5. renewal of road vegetation in continuous sections

- 3. REPAIRS

Repairs primarily include the following works:

- 3.1. strengthening or widening of carriageway and verges

- 3.2. construction of pavements, their strengthening, or raising of the grade line

- 3.3. removal of landslides, rock stabilization in cuts and excavations

- 3.4. removal of mining damage to the road body

- 3.5. repair of the road crown including components and accessories

- 3.6. renewal of structures listed in point 1.7.

PLANNING AND ORGANIZATION OF WINTER MAINTENANCE

- 1. Winter maintenance plan for motorways

Motorway winter maintenance plans are prepared in two stages:

- operational plan of individual motorway administration and maintenance centers (hereinafter referred to as „SSÚD“)

- operational plan of the organization; the organization submits the plan to the Ministry of Transport and Communications by October 20.

- 1.1. The SSÚD plan includes:

- a) a map of the motorway section at a scale of 1:50,000, indicating:

- motorway sections maintained by spreading chemical de-icing materials (red color), by spraying chemical solutions (blue color), and by spreading abrasive materials (yellow color)

- locations of winter maintenance machinery and routes of work circuits

- storage facilities for abrasive spreading materials over 1000 m^3 (letters IP), chemical de-icing materials over 100 t (letters CHRL), and chemical solutions (letters CHR)

- distribution of snow fences along the motorway (brown color)

- telephone connections (letter T)

- locations of radiotelephone fixed stations (letter V)

- fuel depots and filling stations (letters PH – petrol, diesel)

- location of ice detector probes (letters HN)

- b) a text part, which shall state:

- a list of employees responsible for mitigating defects in road passability and accessibility of roadways, parking lots, sidewalks, and for information services, including their addresses and telephone connections

- a list of employees assigned to winter service, including their addresses and telephone connections

- a list of contractually secured employees

- a list of important telephone and fax connections to senior employees of the organization, other SSÚDs, the central state administration body, the Police of the Czech Republic, healthcare facilities, etc.

- a list of motorway sections, intersections, and maintained rest areas

- a list of motorway sections maintained by spreading chemical de-icing materials, chemical solutions, and abrasive spreading materials

- organization of winter maintenance intervention – prepared in variants according to characteristic climatic and weather conditions in the winter period in the given motorway section

- organization of motorway passability status control

- organization of shifts and their management

- inventory of storage facilities for spreading and liquid materials for winter maintenance and their sources

- inventory of material depots, fuel depots, and filling stations

- inventory of machinery locations, specifying types and numbers of machinery

- description of accommodation and catering conditions for winter maintenance workers

- data on agreed assistance in emergency situations

- method of obtaining meteorological reports and forecasts.

- 1.2. The organization's plan includes:

- a) a map of maintained motorways at a scale of 1:50,000, indicating individual sections and SSÚDs

- b) a tabular overview of machinery and equipment for winter maintenance, numbers of employees designated for winter maintenance, and quantities of spreading and liquid materials according to individual SSÚDs

- c) data on agreed assistance during emergency situations

- d) a list of the organization's employees responsible for motorway winter maintenance, including telephone connections and addresses

- e) the method of organizing and ensuring information services

- f) the anticipated method of assistance by individual SSÚDs, including contact information for senior SSÚD employees

- g) the winter maintenance regime in protected landscape areas, in drinking water source areas, on bridges according to recommended ČSN 73 6221.

- 2. Winter maintenance plan for roads

The road winter maintenance plan is submitted by the road administrator to the relevant administrative authority for an opinion and by September 30 to the workplace authorized by the Ministry of Transport and Communications. The plan consists of the following parts:

- 2.1. Map section:

- a) Map of the maintained network at a scale of 1:100,000, indicating the order of importance of winter service as follows:

I. order - red

II. order - blue

III. order - yellow

unmaintained - no designation

- b) Map of spreading machinery routes under usual winter conditions is prepared with regard to the order of importance of roads at a scale of 1:50,000. This map shall indicate:

- routes (circuits) of spreaders - a solid, distinct colored line for each route with an abbreviation expressing:

CH - spreading with chemical de-icing material

Z - abrasive spreading

1 - ... - district number

V - performed by own capacity

D - performed by contractor

(example: CH 12 - V )

- roads maintained only by plowing - a dashed colored line

- unmaintained roads - no designation

- c) Map with determination of plowing routes at a scale of 1:100,000 or 1:50,000.

- 2.2. Text section:

- a) Persons responsible for winter maintenance.

Responsibility for winter maintenance is determined by the organizational rules of the road administrator and the job descriptions of senior employees.

Responsible for the performance of winter maintenance are:

- throughout the entire administered territory: the head of winter maintenance, outside working hours the shift leader of winter maintenance

- in the roadmaster's district: during working hours the roadmaster, outside working hours an authorized employee (dispatcher, shift leader)

- on a route maintained from home standby: the driver to whom the route is assigned and who is on duty at the given time according to the regular schedule. Any changes are recorded in the winter maintenance log at the center by the roadmaster.
